spec_match.rationale: The header now uses valid CSS Values 5 progress(value, start, end) syntax wrapped in clamp() to keep the scroll-derived ratio bounded.

interactive.rationale: The scroller and fallback range input both update the same header tint, font size, and progress bar using live custom properties.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The copy now accurately explains that JavaScript supplies --scroll-y while CSS progress() performs the normalization for visual properties.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo handles unsupported progress() with a no-native-progress fallback and clamps below-start and above-end scroll offsets to the intended 0..1 range.

references.rationale: The page links to the feature index, progress() basics, ChromeStatus, and the CSS Values 5 progress() section.

design_system.rationale: The broken color arithmetic is replaced with color-mix() over shared design tokens, and the touched page has no raw color literals or inline event handlers.

summary: Resolved the broken header color critique by replacing invalid CSS arithmetic with valid color-mix() formulas, using spec-current comma progress() syntax, bounding the ratio with clamp(), and preserving a working fallback path.
